title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Main
Sentiment in the freight market has weakened further for smaller ship sizes, while the Capesize segment is still in a recovery phase. In the iron ore market, prices picked up again on Wednesday. According to Fastmarkets MB, the 62% Fe benchmark imported to North China changed hands for $108.91/tonne, up 3.72%.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Sentiment in the freight market has weakened further for smaller ship sizes, while the Capesize segment is still in a recovery phase. In the iron ore market, prices picked up again on Wednesday.

children.title: Chart of The Week: Dry Bulk Flows, Coal, to India: Record high in June
section_path: Chart of The Week: Dry Bulk Flows, Coal, to India: Record high in June
children.section_path_text: Chart of The Week: Dry Bulk Flows, Coal, to India: Record high in June
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (38).png | assets/2022-07-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed283829_fb611bcf8101.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows, Coal, from ALL Countries to India, 2021-2022

children.title: SECTION 1 - FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) - Weaker
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
Image reference: unnamed (39).png | assets/2022-07-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed283929_088ee47e0af5.png
The Capesize segment showed a sustained upward trend, while the other vessel categories continued to show a downward trend over the last three weeks. Capesize Brazil-to-North China freight rates held at a level of about $31/tonne with an upward trend.

children.title: Number of Vessels - Increasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: unnamed (40).png | assets/2022-07-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed284029_3ccbfea69c20.png
The number of ships sailing ballast increased sharply in the Supramax segment, while there was a sudden drop in Handysize. Capesize SE Africa: The number of vessels increased to 73, 7 more than week 26.

children.title: Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (41).png | assets/2022-07-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed284129_0a13d8d6ca46.png
A downward trend emerged in the second week of July, with the Handysize and Capesize segments showing significantly lower growth. Capesize demand ton-days: The percentage increase this week shows a downward trend and disappoints expectations for an upturn in July.

children.title: Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
section_path: Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (42).png | assets/2022-07-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed284229_af668fbab458.png
The number of congested vessels has remained excessive since the end of June. Capesize: The number of congested vessels increased to 130, 7 more than the previous week.
